notes: Roman emperor from 180 to 192 CE, known for cruelty and tyranny, son of Marcus Aurelius.

docs.title: Life of Clodius Albinus
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … that Commodus was hated because of his evil ways, which were bringing destruction upon the state and dishonour upon himself, and that he would sometime or other be slain, and he feared that he might perish with him.

docs.title: Life of Commodus
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … he was base and dishonourable, and cruel and lewd, defiled of mouth, moreover, and debauched.⁠ 3 8 Even then he was an adept in certain arts which are not becoming in an emperor , for he could mould goblets and dance and sing and whistle, and he could play the buffoon and the gladiator to

docs.title: Life of Marcus Aurelius
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: 183 28 1 He died in the following manner:⁠ 184 When he began to grow ill, he summoned his son and besought him first of all not to think lightly of what remained of the war, lest he seem a traitor to the state.
